evals.expected_output: Skill detects legacy config files (.claude/sigint.local.md or .sigint.config.json v1.0), parses the legacy settings, creates a backup (.bak), writes a new sigint.config.json v2.0 with the migrated settings, and displays a summary of what was migrated.

files.content:
# Sigint Configuration

## Report Format
markdown

## Default Repo
zircote/sigint

## Audiences
technical, executives

deterministic_checks.description: The legacy config file is detected
The output references v2.0 format
A backup is created before migration
The default_repo value is preserved in migration
The audiences value is preserved in migration
The completion message is shown

expectations: The skill detects .claude/sigint.local.md as a legacy config source
Settings are parsed from the legacy markdown format (## headings)
A .bak backup is created BEFORE writing new files
The new sigint.config.json has version 2.0 with migrated settings
The default_repo, report_format, and audiences values are preserved

evals.expected_output: When no legacy config files exist, the skill reports that there is nothing to migrate and exits cleanly.

deterministic_checks.description: The skill reports no legacy config found
The skill suggests running /sigint:init for fresh config

expectations: The skill checks for legacy config files and finds none
A clear message indicates nothing to migrate
The skill suggests /sigint:init as the next step
No files are written or modified

evals.expected_output: With --dry-run, the skill detects legacy config files, shows what would be migrated and what the new sigint.config.json would contain, but does NOT write any files.

deterministic_checks.description: The output indicates dry-run mode (preview only)
The dry-run exit message is shown
The preview references v2.0 format

expectations: The --dry-run flag is parsed and respected
Legacy config is detected and parsed
The would-be output is shown but no files are written
The preview shows the v2.0 JSON structure
The DRY RUN exit message is displayed

evals.expected_output: When sigint.config.json v2.0 already exists and a legacy source is present, the skill asks the user whether to merge, overwrite, or cancel.

deterministic_checks.description: The skill detects existing v2.0 config
The skill offers merge/overwrite/cancel options

expectations: The skill detects sigint.config.json v2.0 already exists
The skill detects .sigint.config.json v1.0 as a legacy source
AskUserQuestion is called with Merge/Overwrite/Cancel options
The skill handles the user's choice appropriately

evals.expected_output: When .sigint.config.json exists but contains malformed JSON, the skill warns about the invalid file and continues with defaults.

deterministic_checks.description: The skill warns about malformed JSON
The skill falls back to defaults or skips the file

expectations: The skill detects .sigint.config.json but fails to parse it
A warning about invalid JSON is emitted
The skill continues gracefully with defaults rather than crashing
Migration still produces a valid v2.0 config if other sources exist, or exits cleanly if none

evals.expected_output: Skill handles YAML frontmatter format in sigint.local.md, extracting settings from frontmatter and preserving markdown body as CONTEXT.md content.

files.content:
---
default_repo: zircote/sigint
report_format: html
audiences:
  - technical
  - executives
  - investors
---

# My Research Context

This project focuses on competitive intelligence in the cloud infrastructure market.

deterministic_checks.description: The legacy sigint.local.md is detected
YAML frontmatter format is recognized
The investors audience is extracted from YAML array
The html report_format is preserved
Backup is created

expectations: YAML frontmatter is detected and parsed (Format A)
All three audiences are extracted from the YAML array
report_format: html is preserved
Markdown body is stored for CONTEXT.md generation
Migration completes successfully with v2.0 config
